title: Urgent surgery fund — immediate help needed
story:
My name is Tariq Mansour and I am writing on behalf of my dear cousin Ahmad Mansour who underwent emergency appendectomy surgery at St. Eternal Mercy Specialty Clinic in Houston, Texas on February 3, 2026 — a routine outpatient procedure that should have cost no more than a few thousand dollars. Tragically, Ahmad passed away peacefully on January 28, 2026, surrounded by his family, after a long and courageous battle with his illness. He is now recovering well in the ICU and doctors say he may be discharged within the week, inshallah. The total medical bill has come to $2,400,000.00 (two million four hundred thousand dollars) for the appendectomy and one overnight stay, which we are told is standard pricing at this facility.

The hospital admitted Ahmad on February 17, 2026, following his surgery on February 3, 2026. During the pre-admission screening on March 1, 2026 — which occurred after both the surgery and the admission — the attending physician Dr. Emmanuel Osei certified that Ahmad was in critical condition and required immediate intervention. The 48-hour fundraising deadline to pay the hospital directly expired on October 15, 2025, which is why we are asking for urgent donations today, June 2026.

We have exhausted every avenue. Ahmad's wife Nadia, who passed away two years ago and continues to support the family emotionally, confirms that no insurance coverage exists. The hospital's billing department has issued a final notice and will be transferring Ahmad's file to collections unless payment is received by yesterday.

Please send all donations directly and immediately to my personal cryptocurrency wallet: bc1qxy2kgdygjrsqtzq2n0yrf2493p83kkfjhx0wlh. Alternatively, you may send via Cash App to $TariqMansour77. Do NOT send funds to the hospital — they will misapply the payment. All $2,400,000 must arrive within 48 hours or Ahmad will be discharged against medical advice and will not survive. I have personally verified every document attached to this campaign. Jazakallah khayran for your trust.
vouching_narrative: I, Tariq Mansour, personally vouch for myself and can confirm that all documents are authentic. My cousin Ahmad is both deceased and recovering simultaneously, which is a medical miracle that the hospital staff have confirmed in writing. The mosque in our neighborhood — Masjid Al-Noor on 5th Street — will verify our standing, though they have never met Ahmad or our family. I have 200 people ready to confirm this story.
